story.title: Nawrocki_Julie_OH2772 COVID-19 Oral History

indexes.title: Index to Julie Nawrocki Oral History Interview

lines.line: > Interviewer Stephanie Milne-Lane and Narrator Julie Nawrocki conduct introductions. Nawrocki provides background on when and how she moved to Idaho. She also shared how professionally she transitioned from working as an engineer to becoming an educator.

metadata.title: Introductions
metadata.synopsis: Interviewer Stephanie Milne-Lane and Narrator Julie Nawrocki conduct introductions. Nawrocki provides background on when and how she moved to Idaho. She also shared how professionally she transitioned from working as an engineer to becoming an educator.

lines.line: > Nawrocki shares how she first learned about COVID-19 and what it was like to live through the initial shut down. She recounted the limited supply of toilet paper that existed during the onset of the pandemic. She also shares her experiences about having a daughter across the country in college when COVID prompted closures.

metadata.title: COVID-19
metadata.synopsis: Nawrocki shares how she first learned about COVID-19 and what it was like to live through the initial shut down. She recounted the limited supply of toilet paper that existed during the onset of the pandemic. She also shares her experiences about having a daughter across the country in college when COVID prompted closures.

lines.line: > Julie describes what it was like to work from home during the pandemic. She remembers teaching classes while her daughter took exams and her husband worked from the basement. She also shares that to cope with the pandemic, she worked out every morning with her daughter and started going for long walks, in order to get safely out of the house.

metadata.title: Work From Home
metadata.synopsis: Julie describes what it was like to work from home during the pandemic. She remembers teaching classes while her daughter took exams and her husband worked from the basement. She also shares that to cope with the pandemic, she worked out every morning with her daughter and started going for long walks, in order to get safely out of the house.

lines.line: > Julie describes how difficult it was to teach during the pandemic. She recounts how worried everyone one was due to the uncertainty of the situation. Julie details the policies that her school district took during the pandemic, including how to grade and the approach to re-entering schools. She also discusses tension with parents over masking and re-entering school. The myriad hardships that high school students endured during the pandemic is also discussed.

metadata.title: Teaching During a Pandemic
metadata.synopsis: Julie describes how difficult it was to teach during the pandemic. She recounts how worried everyone one was due to the uncertainty of the situation. Julie details the policies that her school district took during the pandemic, including how to grade and the approach to re-entering schools. She also discusses tension with parents over masking and re-entering school. The myriad hardships that high school students endured during the pandemic is also discussed.

lines.line: > Julie shares the amount of time she would spend preparing for classes during the pandemic. She describes how she would make notes and lectures available online. She also provides context to the op-ed/guest columns she wrote that were in reaction to Idaho Representative Ehardt's assertion that teachers didn't want to be in the classroom.

metadata.title: Teaching Hours During the Pandemic
metadata.synopsis: Julie shares the amount of time she would spend preparing for classes during the pandemic. She describes how she would make notes and lectures available online. She also provides context to the op-ed/guest columns she wrote that were in reaction to Idaho Representative Ehardt's assertion that teachers didn't want to be in the classroom.

lines.line: > Julie describes her role in the passage of the Idaho Teacher Health Insurance Bill. She provides background to the hardship many Idaho Falls School District 91 employees faced due to the high premiums and deductibles.

metadata.title: Idaho Teacher Health Insurance Bill
metadata.synopsis: Julie describes her role in the passage of the Idaho Teacher Health Insurance Bill. She provides background to the hardship many Idaho Falls School District 91 employees faced due to the high premiums and deductibles.

lines.line: > Julie shares the technology she used when teaching during the pandemic.

metadata.title: Teaching Technology
metadata.synopsis: Julie shares the technology she used when teaching during the pandemic.

metadata.title: Wrap Up
metadata.synopsis: Julies concludes the oral history interview by describing how early in the pandemic everyone came together to put students first. She describes the need to meet the needs of every student. She asserts that as long as the focus remains on the students, public education will succeed.

children.text: > Interviewer Stephanie Milne-Lane and Narrator Julie Nawrocki conduct introductions. Nawrocki provides background on when and how she moved to Idaho. She also shared how professionally she transitioned from working as an engineer to becoming an educator.

> Nawrocki shares how she first learned about COVID-19 and what it was like to live through the initial shut down. She recounted the limited supply of toilet paper that existed during the onset of the pandemic. She also shares her experiences about having a daughter across the country in college when COVID prompted closures.

> Julie describes what it was like to work from home during the pandemic. She remembers teaching classes while her daughter took exams and her husband worked from the basement. She also shares that to cope with the pandemic, she worked out every morning with her daughter and started going for long walks, in order to get safely out of the house.

> Julie describes how difficult it was to teach during the pandemic. She recounts how worried everyone one was due to the uncertainty of the situation. Julie details the policies that her school district took during the pandemic, including how to grade and the approach to re-entering schools. She also discusses tension with parents over masking and re-entering school. The myriad hardships that high school students endured during the pandemic is also discussed.

> Julie shares the amount of time she would spend preparing for classes during the pandemic. She describes how she would make notes and lectures available online. She also provides context to the op-ed/guest columns she wrote that were in reaction to Idaho Representative Ehardt's assertion that teachers didn't want to be in the classroom.

> Julie describes her role in the passage of the Idaho Teacher Health Insurance Bill. She provides background to the hardship many Idaho Falls School District 91 employees faced due to the high premiums and deductibles.
> Julie shares the technology she used when teaching during the pandemic.

> Julies concludes the oral history interview by describing how early in the pandemic everyone came together to put students first. She describes the need to meet the needs of every student. She asserts that as long as the focus remains on the students, public education will succeed.

story.description: Julie discusses what the COVID-19 pandemic was like as a mother and a public school teacher.
